task_completion.description: Agent successfully logs into the OpenEMR system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR dashboard is visible, or the login screen is shown with credentials entered followed by a transition to the main interface.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the chart of patient Faye Conn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient name 'Faye Conn' (and potentially DOB 1959-03-16) is visible in the patient header, title bar, or search results.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses the interface for adding a note, alert, or flag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A modal, tab, or form titled 'Add Note', 'Edit Patient', 'Clinical Notes', 'Alerts', or similar is open, allowing text entry.

task_completion.description: Agent enters the specific clinical details regarding difficult venipuncture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text fields contain keywords like 'Difficult Venipuncture', 'scars', 'butterfly needle', 'experienced phlebotomist', or 'right hand dorsum'.

task_completion.description: The alert is saved and appears on the patient's summary screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient dashboard displays the new alert/note with the text 'Difficult Venipuncture' or related instructions visible in the summary view or alerts widget.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the OpenEMR web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser displaying the OpenEMR application, not a terminal or database client.

integrity.description: Agent performs the task within the application, not by simulating output in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is the actual OpenEMR software, not a text file or document editor containing the alert text.

integrity.description: Agent navigates menus rather than pasting a URL or using a script to bypass UI steps.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show navigation through menus (e.g., Search -> Patient -> Edit/Add) rather than sudden jumps to a completed state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Faye Conn (DOB: 1959-03-16). Expected Alert Content: 'Difficult Venipuncture' or 'Difficult IV Access'. Key phrases in note: 'Multiple scars', 'butterfly needle', 'right hand dorsum'.
